title: How to Set Up an Affiliate Program with Creem (Step-by-Step Guide for SaaS Founders)
author: ASH
summary:
Most SaaS founders are doing all the selling themselves.

Content, cold outreach, paid ads, it's exhausting, and it eats into the time you should be spending building. The worst part? You pay for all of it upfront, whether it works or not.

An affiliate program flips that model entirely.

You get other people.

Creators, communities, newsletter writers — to promote your product. And you only pay them when they bring you a paying customer.

No conversions, no cost.

If you're already using Creem as your payment processor, setting up an affiliate program takes less than 10 minutes. Here's exactly how to do it.
full_text:
Most SaaS founders are doing all the selling themselves.

Content, cold outreach, paid ads, it's exhausting, and it eats into the time you should be spending building. The worst part? You pay for all of it upfront, whether it works or not.

An affiliate program flips that model entirely.

You get other people. 

Creators, communities, newsletter writers — to promote your product. And you only pay them when they bring you a paying customer. 

No conversions, no cost.

If you're already using Creem as your payment processor, setting up an affiliate program takes less than 10 minutes. Here's exactly how to do it.

 
 
 Why SaaS Founders Should Set Up an Affiliate Program Early

The best affiliates already have what takes you months to build: an audience, trust, and distribution.

When a creator recommends your product to their audience, it lands differently than an ad. And because you only pay on results, the risk is essentially zero.

That makes affiliate marketing one of the highest-ROI growth channels available to early-stage SaaS founders — and Creem makes it dead simple to launch one without any third-party tools..

Prefer to watch instead of read? I've put together a full video walkthrough of this guide — everything covered in this article, shown live inside the Creem dashboard.

 VIDEO

 
 
 Step 1: Create Your Affiliate Program in Creem

Log into your Creem dashboard and navigate to:

Growth → Affiliate Hub

https://www.creem.io/dashboard/affiliate-hub

If it's your first time, you'll land on the program creation wizard. Click to create your program and fill in the details.

 
 
 Program Name

This is how your program appears to affiliates. Keep it clean and on-brand.

 
 
 Website URL

This is where affiliate traffic lands. Use your main landing page or your highest-converting page — not your homepage if you have something better.

 
 
 Description

Think of this as your pitch to potential affiliates. Answer two things clearly:

What does your product do?

What do they earn for promoting it?

Example:

Earn 30% commission for every customer you refer to [Your Product] — the easiest way for indie developers to accept payments globally.

The better your description, the better quality affiliates you'll attract. Don't skip this.

 
 
 Step 2: Configure Your Slug and Commission Rate

After clicking Next, you'll set up how your program actually works.

 
 
 Program Slug

Your slug powers every referral link your affiliates share. For example, if your slug is yourproduct, affiliate links will look like:

?ref=yourproduct

Keep it short, readable, and consistent with your brand.

 
 
 Commission Rate

This is the percentage of each sale you pay to affiliates. For SaaS, the typical range is 20% to 30%. If you're early-stage and want to attract higher-quality affiliates faster, consider starting at the higher end — you can always adjust later.

Once you're happy with the settings, click Create Program.

Your affiliate program is now live. No integrations needed. No extra tools.

 
 
 Step 3: Understanding Your Affiliate Hub Dashboard

Once your program is created, you'll land on the Affiliate Hub dashboard. This is your command center for tracking everything.

Here's what you can monitor:

Revenue — Total revenue generated through affiliate referrals

Unique Leads — Unique visitors coming from affiliate links

Total Clicks — All clicks across all affiliate links

Checkouts Created — Checkout sessions started by referred visitors

Conversion Rate — The percentage of leads that become paying customers

Affiliates — Number of active partners in your program

It'll be empty at first — but once affiliates start sharing, this is where everything compounds.

 
 
 Step 4: Invite Your First Affiliates

From the Affiliate Hub, click Invite Partner.

You'll need to enter:

Their email address

Their name

Click Send Invite

Creem handles the entire email flow automatically. The affiliate receives an invitation with a link to join your program, and their invite will show as "Pending" in your Partners list until they accept.

You don't need to set up email sequences or manage this manually — it's all handled out of the box.

Who should you invite first? Think about people who already use your product and love it, creators in your niche with small but engaged audiences, or founders in adjacent spaces who share your target customer.

 
 
 Step 5: What Your Affiliate Sees

Once invited, your affiliate joins through the dedicated affiliate portal at:

affiliates.creem.io

They can sign in using Google or a magic link sent to the email you invited them with.

Inside their dashboard, they get a full view of their performance:

Their unique referral link to share

Revenue earned from referrals

Customers referred

Total clicks on their links

Conversion rate

A 30-day commission chart showing earnings over time

Their referral link is their main tool. Every click, lead, and sale is tracked through it automatically.

 
 
 Step 6: How Affiliate Tracking Works

Each affiliate gets a unique referral link that looks like this:

https://creem.io/affiliate?code=XXXX

When someone clicks that link, here's what happens:

Creem tracks which affiliate referred the visitor

The user is redirected to your website

A cookie is stored in their browser

If the user makes a purchase within the cookie window (typically 30–90 days, set by you), the affiliate gets credited for that sale — automatically.

This means affiliates don't need to worry about instant conversions. If someone clicks their link today, browses around, and comes back three weeks later to buy, the commission still applies.

 
 
 Step 7: How Payouts Work

Affiliates can track their earnings directly from their dashboard under the Balance page, which shows three states:

Available Balance — Funds ready to withdraw

On Hold — Pending verification or review

Pending Payouts — Payout requests currently being processed

Before an affiliate can request their first payout, they need to complete two things:

1. Identity Verification (KYC)

Creem uses Sumsub for identity verification. This requires a government-issued ID and a selfie. Verification typically completes within 24 hours.

2. Payout Method Setup

Affiliates can choose between:

Bank Transfer via Paysway

Cryptocurrency (USDC) via Mural

Once verified, they can request payouts directly from the dashboard. Processing typically takes 3–5 business days after the request is submitted.

No spreadsheets. No manual tracking. No chasing people for payment details.

 
 
 Step 8: Test Before You Go Live

Before inviting affiliates, run a quick test to make sure everything is working end-to-end.

Open your affiliate referral link

Visit your website through it

Complete a test purchase

Check your dashboard

You should see the conversion reflected in your stats. Check from the affiliate side too — their revenue and conversion data should update.

It takes five minutes and saves you headaches later.

 
 
 Step 9: Program Defaults to Know

A couple of things worth knowing before you go live:

Programs require manual approval by default. New affiliates who try to join won't get in automatically — you'll need to approve them. This is actually a good thing early on; it lets you vet who's promoting your product.

Programs are private by default. Affiliates can only join if you invite them directly. If you want to open it up so anyone can apply, you can change this in your program settings.

 
 
 A Few Limitations to Keep in Mind

Creem's affiliate program is excellent for early-stage founders, but there are a couple of things to be aware of:

Per-affiliate commission customization — Currently, the commission rate applies across all affiliates in a program. You can't set a different rate for individual affiliates yet. For most early-stage founders, this isn't a problem — one rate keeps things simple. But as you scale and bring on higher-value partners, having per-affiliate rate control would be a valuable addition. This feels like a natural next step for Creem to add.

Product-level affiliate restrictions — Right now, there's limited control over which specific products an affiliate can promote. Again, not an issue when you're starting out, but worth knowing. Custom per-product affiliate rules would make this platform even more powerful for larger catalogs.

For most indie founders and early SaaS teams, these limitations won't slow you down. The simplicity is actually the point — you don't need complexity in the beginning. You need distribution.

That said, these are minor limitations — both are straightforward features Creem could easily ship. No-brainer additions for a future update.

 
 
 Final Thoughts

If you're already processing payments through Creem, turning on the affiliate program is a no-brainer. It takes under 10 minutes to set up, requires zero additional tools, and creates a growth channel that pays for itself.

Start small. Invite five to ten people who already love your product. Watch how the referral data compounds.

The best time to set up your affiliate program is before you need it — so that when you're ready to scale, the infrastructure is already in place.
